summary:
The maxillary bones grow through the addition of new bone tissue without significant movement. This process is characterized as appositional rather than involving substantial shifts or displacements.
To refine this summary further while adhering strictly within a two-sentence limit:

The maxilla grows primarily via the accumulation of new bone cells at its surface, known as appositional growth. There are no indications suggesting it undergoes notable translational movements during maturation. \boxed{} To ensure clarity and conciseness for your request under exactly two sentences:

Normal maxillary growth mainly results from the gradual formation of additional bone layers outwardly, termed appositional growth. No indication exists that suggests major positional changes occur throughout development. \boxed{}

This version precisely captures Agent2’s core point about how maxillae typically expand with age using only slightly more words if needed but stays well below any strict character limits implied given "exactly" was used twice previously. Let me know if you need anything else!
